name: Bank
overviewContent:
# Bank — Overview

**Environment:** Urban bank in Los Angeles, California; heavily fortified Prestige National Bank downtown financial institution
**Floors:** Basement/0F, Ground Floor/1F, Top Floor/2F + Exterior & Roof
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season
**Year Added / Reworked:** Y1 S1 (2015, original); full art rework Y6S3 Crystal Guard (September 2021)

> 📌 **Y11S1 Note:** Bank remains in the ranked pool for the full Y11S1 season. The map emphasizes verticality and hatch play — key strengths for coordinated team play.

---

## Overview

Bank is a verticality-heavy map centered around the three-story Prestige National Bank building in Los Angeles. The map emphasizes hatch play and multi-floor coordination, with distinctive features including the iconic Skylight hatch above 2F CEO Office, basement Vault with single-entry defense, and complex basement corridor network. Bank rewards coordinated vertical pressure and careful destruction sequencing. Four bomb site options create flexible strategies. The Crystal Guard rework (Y6S3) significantly altered exterior angles, removed vault metal bars for improved destruction, blocked Low Roof skylights, adjusted Back Alley sightlines, and updated spawn positions. The map remains a cornerstone competitive selection.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1 — CEO Office / Teller's Office — 2F
**Rooms:** CEO Office and Teller's Office
**Description:** 2F bomb site with two distinct rooms. CEO Office is a giant central room with large desk; directly vulnerable to Skylight hatch above and floor destruction from 1F Archives. Teller's Office is a long room featuring bank teller counter windows with views into 1F Tellers; destructible walls connect to main circulation and floor destruction enables 1F Teller sightlines. Surrounded by destructible walls and doors to Executive Lounge (west), Exec Hallway (north), and Janitor (south).
**Common defense:** Anchor holds CEO behind desk for strong cover; Executive Lounge anchor supports via door angles. Secondary anchor can position in Teller's counter for window angles. Roamers control Banana hallway and Stock Hallway for stairwell/vertical pressure denial. Multiple reinforcements needed to segment objective.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_CEO_Tellers.md

### Site 2 — Archives / Open Space — 1F
**Rooms:** Archives (large north-center room) and Open Space (large central room)
**Description:** 1F bomb site featuring two distinct large rooms with multiple approach vectors. Archives is positioned north with connections to Square and main hallways; destructible floor enables 2F CEO pressure. Open Space is the central room connected to Kitchen, Printer, Admin through multiple doors/destructible walls; floor hatch drops to 0F Lockers Hallway. Each room has distinct entry vectors and rotations.
**Common defense:** Anchor positioning varies — can hold from Archive corners or Open Space center depending on objective. Roamers position in Square, Lobby corners, and main stairwell areas. Atrium/Square becomes critical roamer zone for all 1F sites.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Staff_OpenArea.md

### Site 3 — Tellers / Tellers' Office — 1F
**Rooms:** Tellers' Office (long counter room) and Tellers (curved teller counter area)
**Description:** 1F features teller-focused bomb objective locations. Tellers' Office is long room with curved teller windows and connection to Lobby; destructible floor enables 2F Teller sightlines. Tellers curved area is the exposed teller counter with direct Lobby connection. Primary access from Banana stairs via floor destruction or Lobby approach.
**Common defense:** Anchor positioning holds from Teller's counter windows. Roamers manage Lobby corners and Banana stairwell. Window angles into Lobby critical for defense.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Archives_Tellers.md

### Site 4 — Vault / Server Room — Basement (0F)
**Rooms:** Vault (heavily reinforced room) and Server Room (large northeast room with pillars)
**Description:** Basement features vault-focused and server-focused bomb objective locations. Vault is iconic heavily-reinforced room with single red-carpeted entrance via Vault Entrance room; minimal destruction options. Server Room is large northeast room with pillars and cover; accessible from basement corridor network and ceiling hatches. Ceiling hatches from 1F Open Space, Admin, and Elevators enable vertical pressure on all basement sites.
**Common defense:** Vault anchor holds exterior door with angled coverage; single entry makes it most defensible basement location. Server Room anchor positions among pillars for cover. Roamers manage basement corridor network (Red Hallway, Lockers Hallway) and upper basement stairwell monitoring.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Servers_CCTV.md

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Top Floor) — Verified names

|------|---------|-------|
| CEO Office | CEO | Bomb site (Site 1A). Large central room. Skylight hatch above. |

| Exec Hallway | Hallway / Exec Hall | North connector to CEO. |
| 2F Banana | Banana | Curved hallway above 1F Tellers. Destructible floor. |

### Basement (0F) — Verified names

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls (prioritize reinforcing)
- **Vault Entry Door (Basement):** Single point of entry to Vault bomb site; heavily fortified; primary defense anchor
- **CEO to Executive Lounge wall (2F):** Separates primary bomb room from support room; divides site defense
- **CEO to Exec Hallway wall (2F):** Controls north entrance to CEO; key chokepoint
- **Server Room walls (Basement):** Multiple destruction walls around Server; reinforce to prevent wrapping
- **Lockers to surrounding rooms (Basement):** Multiple reinforcements control Lockers bomb site access
- **Tellers' Office walls (1F):** Destruction walls to main areas; control access to teller objective

### Key Destructible/Soft Walls (critical for vertical play)
- **Skylight hatch opening above CEO Office (2F):** Massive destructible hatch; primary vertical pressure point; usually destroyed early
- **CEO Office floor (2F):** Destructible floor enables gunfights to 1F Archives
- **Tellers' Office floor (2F Banana):** Curved hallway floor directly above 1F Tellers; destructible for vertical angles
- **Tellers' Office floor (1F):** Destructible roof enables 2F Teller sightlines
- **Archives floor (1F):** Destructible roof to 2F CEO; critical vertical connection
- **Top Square floor (2F):** Destructible floor to 1F Square; vertical pressure point
- **Open Space roof (1F):** Destructible ceiling to 2F Stock; vertical destruction opportunity
- **Stock Trading Room floor (2F):** Destructible floor to 1F Open Space
- **Admin ceiling (1F):** Destructible to 2F Janitor
- **Red Hallway walls (Basement):** Longitudinal and latitudinal destruction walls; basement corridor control
- **Lockers Hallway connections (Basement):** Destruction walls to Lockers/Red Hallway/CCTV

### Key Hatches (8 total)
- **Skylight hatch (above 2F CEO):** Massive vertical pressure point; roof access
- **2F Banana to 1F Tellers (destructible floor):** Mid-map vertical connection; roamer rotation route
- **Top Square to 1F Square (destructible floor/staircase hatch):** Large stairwell; vertical access
- **1F Open Space to 0F Lockers Hallway (floor hatch):** Basement vertical pressure point
- **1F Admin to 0F CCTV (floor hatch):** Admin area basement connection
- **1F Elevators to 0F Elevators (floor/ceiling hatches):** Vertical elevator shaft access
- **2F Elevators to 1F Elevators (ceiling hatch):** West elevator only; vertical connection
- **1F Square to 0F Server (ceiling hatch):** Server room vertical access; critical basement pressure

---

name: Border
overviewContent:
# Border — Overview

**Environment:** Middle East military border checkpoint facility; sand/interior mix with open air pathways
**Floors:** 2 (1F, 2F) + Exterior rooftops and catwalks
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season only
**Year Added / Reworked:** Added Y2S1 (2016); major rework in Y5S3 (Crimson Heist, 2020); Y11S1 mid-season updates

> 📌 **Y11S1 Note:** Border is in the first-half Y11S1 ranked pool. The stack's exceptional Border performance (Sarge 76.9%, all players 57%+) makes this a high-priority protect/keep map early in the season before mid-season pool swap.

---

## Overview

Border is a two-story Middle East military checkpoint facility with emphasis on exterior access and multi-directional attack routes. The map is highly destructible with open air pathways creating constant pressure from multiple directions. Border's defining characteristic is openness and multiple exterior entry points (East Vehicle Entrance, Valley, West Vehicle Exit) enabling coordinated multi-angle attacks. The map features prominent vertical stairwell access (Yellow Stairs central, Red Stairs east) and 2F Security Room hatches providing dual vertical pressure vectors into 1F Customs areas. The Crimson Heist rework (Y5S3) introduced layout changes including 1F Bathroom extension, Supply Room relocation, reduced soft walls in Passport Check area, 2F Armory Lockers redesign, and extended 2F Archives/Offices configuration. Border is the stack's strongest map with dominant win rates across all players (57%-76.9%) and deep strategic understanding of site control and exterior pressure.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1 — Armory Lockers / Archives — 2F
**Rooms:** Armory Lockers (1A) / Archives (1B)
**Common Attack Approach:** Yellow Stairs primary approach from Main Lobby; exterior catwalks secondary; exterior window pressure
**Common Defense:** Stair denial with Kapkan at Yellow Stairs top; Aruni gate at north site entries; roamer pressure from Break Room area
**Key Feature:** North 2F site with Red/Yellow Stairs as approach vectors. Soft floor above Main Lobby enables vertical pressure from below.
**Attack Strategy:** See ATK_Armory_Archives.md
**Defense Strategy:** See DEF_Armory_Archives.md

### Site 2 — Offices / Security Room — 2F
**Rooms:** Offices (2A) / Security Room (2B)
**Common Attack Approach:** Red Stairs primary; East Entrance secondary; dual hatch destruction from above creates vertical splits
**Common Defense:** Anchor holds Security Room with deep position; roamer from Admin watches Long Hallway approaches; hatch monitors for vertical pressure
**Key Feature:** TWO hatches from Security Room down to 1F Customs Inspection AND Customs Desk — enables simultaneous vertical pressure from 2F to 1F
**Attack Strategy:** See ATK_Offices_Security.md
**Defense Strategy:** See DEF_Offices_Security.md

### Site 3 — Workshop / Ventilation — 1F
**Rooms:** Workshop (3A) / Ventilation (3B)
**Common Attack Approach:** Main Lobby entry (west side); Yellow Stairs from north; exterior windows
**Common Defense:** Anchor holds Workshop machinery cover; Ventilation as secondary position or roamer trap; vertical defenders manage 2F hatch pressure
**Key Feature:** West 1F industrial site. Yellow Stairs provides north approach. Soft floor destructibility creates vertical pressure opportunity from 2F above.
**Attack Strategy:** See ATK_Workshop_Ventilation.md (⚠️ Partially Developed)
**Defense Strategy:** See DEF_Workshop_Ventilation.md

### Site 4 — Customs Inspection / Tellers — 1F
**Rooms:** Customs Inspection (4A) / Tellers (4B)
**Common Attack Approach:** Main Entrance primary (south); Red Stairs from north; Bathroom flank (post-rework)
**Common Defense:** Anchor holds Customs counter; Tellers secondary; roamer from Passport Check/Armory intercepts; Bathroom extension creates additional anchor option
**Key Feature:** East 1F administrative site. Red Stairs and Security Room hatches above provide vertical pressure vectors. Bathroom extension (post-rework) connects from west.
**Attack Strategy:** See ATK_Customs_Tellers.md (❌ Not Developed)
**Defense Strategy:** See DEF_Customs_Tellers.md

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Top Floor) — Verified names

### 1F (Ground Floor) — Verified names

### Exterior & Spawns

| East exterior side | East Side / East Ext | Right-side building exterior |
| West exterior side | West Side / West Ext | Left-side building exterior |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Armory Lockers entries (2F):** Multiple walls segmenting north site from central areas
- **Archives ↔ Offices (2F):** Divides bomb objectives; primary reinforcement
- **Security Room walls (2F):** Primary site protection; hatch floors remain critical
- **Red Stairs landings (both floors):** Vertical access control points
- **Customs Inspection entry (1F):** Funnel attacker pressure through controlled entry
- **Workshop/Ventilation walls (1F):** West site compartmentalization

### Key Destructible/Soft Walls
- **Yellow Stairs floor/ceiling:** Central vertical destructible passage between 1F and 2F (CRITICAL)
- **2F floors over 1F:** Multiple destructible ceiling points enable vertical pressure
- **Passport Check area:** Soft walls reduced from 4 to 2 in Crimson Heist; identify remaining breach opportunities
- **Bathroom ↔ Tellers wall (1F):** Extended bathroom creates new soft connection (post-rework)
- **Main Lobby walls:** Soft destruction opportunities creating rotation access

### Key Hatches (5 total)
- **Security Room ↔ Customs Inspection (2F→1F):** Vertical pressure from Security Room above into Customs desk area
- **Security Room ↔ Customs Desk (2F→1F):** Second hatch from Security Room to ground Customs
- **Yellow Stairs vertical:** Destructible floor between 1F Main Lobby and 2F center
- **Red Stairs vertical:** Destructible passage between 1F Tellers and 2F east areas
- **Additional vertical connections:** Between floors for roamer access and pressure

---

name: Chalet
overviewContent:
# Chalet — Overview

---

## Summary

Chalet is a three-story luxury French Alps chalet with heavy emphasis on interior anchor positions and vertical roamer play. The map features 4 bomb sites across three floors: Bedroom/Office and Bar/Gaming on upper floors, Dining/Kitchen on 1F, and Wine Cellar/Snowmobile in the basement.

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Rooms | Floor | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|-------|
| **Site 1** | Bedroom / Office | 2F | Large residential + office space. Strong anchor positions with furniture cover. |
| **Site 2** | Bar / Gaming | 1F | Bar counter + entertainment area. Moderate anchor points. |
| **Site 3** | Dining / Kitchen | 1F | Dining with tables + food prep. Split interior spaces. |
| **Site 4** | Wine Cellar / Snowmobile | B | Basement sites. Isolated, defender-biased access. |

---

## Map Characteristics

### Vertical Structure
- **2F:** Bedroom/Office site on east side; Library/Piano area on west. Tight corridors connect upper floor. Library Stairs primary descent.
- **1F:** Three distinct circulation zones (Bar/Games west, Dining/Kitchen east, Fireplace central). Multiple staircase access points. Heavy furniture and tight spaces.
- **Basement:** Wine Cellar/Snowmobile sites separated by Red/Connector area. Isolated from upper floors; defensible position.

### Key Features
- **Tight corridors throughout:** Entire map emphasizes narrow hallways and confined spaces—defender-favorable for tight angles.
- **Strong anchor rooms:** Bedroom (2F), Bar (1F), Wine/Snowmobile (B) all offer excellent furniture cover and defensible positions.
- **Multi-floor vertical roaming:** Multiple staircase access points (Library, Lobby, Trophy, Wine, Snowmobile stairs) enable fast roamer rotations between floors.
- **Split 1F sites:** Two bomb sites on same floor (Dining, Bar/Gaming) requires defenders to manage dual-pressure scenarios.
- **Basement isolation:** Wine/Snowmobile basement area is naturally defender-biased due to limited attacker access routes.

name: Clubhouse
overviewContent:
# Clubhouse — Overview

**Environment:** German biker gang clubhouse ("Die Vier Reiter"/"The Four Horsemen") in Hannover; featuring bar, gaming, and mixed interior/exterior layout
**Floors:** Basement / Underground (B), Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor (2F) + Roof
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season only
**Year Added / Reworked:** Y1 original; major interior rework Y3S1 (Operation Para Bellum); minor refinements Y6S3 (Crystal Guard)

---

## Overview

Clubhouse is a three-story German biker gang clubhouse with strong interior design, mixed layout, and multiple bomb objectives. The map depicts a GSG9 raid on Die Vier Reiter in Hannover, with a bar area as the focal point. Para Bellum interior rework (Y3S1) significantly updated map flow; Crystal Guard made minor refinements. Clubhouse features destructible floors, 7 hatches, 4 attacker spawns (Main Gate, Shipping Dock, Warehouse, Construction Site), and extensive rotation/mobility routes across multiple vertical and horizontal play options.

The stack shows strong fundamentals on Clubhouse with most players maintaining 57–67% win rate, indicating solid familiarity and coordinated play. The team has developed 1 partial strategy (ATK_Gym_Bedroom from STACK_03) with 3 bomb sites underdeveloped, suggesting emerging comfort with the map or incomplete strategic preparation. Recommend completing remaining 3 site strategies to maximize win rate ceiling.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1 — Gym / Bedroom — 2F
**Rooms:** Gym (1A — open fitness space) / Bedroom (1B — residential room with cover)
**Description:** 2F north-side bomb site. Relatively open compared to other Clubhouse sites; Gym provides sparse cover while Bedroom offers better anchor furniture. Multiple windows create exterior pressure vectors. Staircase and hatch access from below. Site is frequently attacked early due to accessibility.
**Common defense:** Defenders hold both rooms with depth positioning; Bedroom furniture offers better anchor coverage. Roamers from adjacent Storage/Corridor area provide pressure. Deployable shields and utility in entry corridors slow attacker access.
**Attack approach:** Multi-staircase routes with utility denial. Soft wall destruction between Gym and adjacent rooms creates new plant angles. Early entry due to map positioning makes this a team-comfort site.

### Site 2 — CCTV / Cash Room — 2F
**Rooms:** CCTV (2A — technical room) / Cash Room (2B — fortress security room)
**Description:** 2F east-side bomb site. Most heavily defensible site on Clubhouse. Cash Room is fortress-like with minimal soft walls and few entry points; CCTV offers technical monitor angles. Site is isolated from exterior pressure due to interior-only access. Requires significant attacker utility commitment to breach.
**Common defense:** Defenders anchor in Cash Room with strong depth positioning; CCTV provides secondary angles. Limited entry points make this a passive hold with strong defender advantage. Roamers from corridors provide early warning.
**Attack approach:** Must commit significant utility to breach Cash Room. Multiple entry angles required from different hallways. Vertical pressure from 1F (ceiling destruction) can split defenders. This site takes longest to breach.

### Site 3 — Bar / Stage — 1F
**Rooms:** Bar (3A — social bar with counter) / Stage (3B — performance/recreation space)
**Description:** 1F northwest bomb site. Moderate size with moderate openness. Bar features counter cover; Stage has furniture and equipment. Accessible from multiple entry routes (Main Entrance, side doors). Exterior walls provide breach opportunities.
**Common defense:** Defenders hold Bar counter as strong anchor with Stage as secondary anchor/depth. Roamers from Kitchen provide early interception and flank denial. Accessible nature requires active, not passive, defense.
**Attack approach:** Push from Main Entrance or side routes into Bar/Stage. Soft wall destruction creates new plant angles. Utility denial prevents roamer repositioning. Moderately difficult attacker execution.

### Site 4 — Arsenal / Garage (Basement) — B
**Rooms:** Arsenal (4A — weapons storage) / Garage Basement (4B — large vehicle storage)
**Description:** Basement bomb site. Isolated vertically with limited access from upper floors (stairs provide tight chokepoint). Features multiple rooms and corridors with maze-like environment. Defender-biased due to limited entry points and underground isolation.
**Common defense:** Defenders position anchors in basement with depth positioning between Arsenal and Garage. Vertical roamers from above pressure attackers descending stairs. Maze layout allows ambush angles; utility denies entry corridors.
**Attack approach:** Careful breach via Basement Stairs (heavily defender-controlled). Vertical pressure from above (ceiling destruction from Kitchen area) can split defenders. Clear basement methodically. Slow, cautious approach required.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Second Floor) — Verified
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Gym | Gym | Bomb site (1A) — open fitness space |
| Bedroom | Bedroom / Bed | Bomb site (1B) — residential area with cover |

| Cash Room | Cash | Bomb site (2B) — fortress security room |
| Storage | Storage / Store | Utility storage area |
| Hallway | 2F Hall / Hallway | Central 2F circulation |
| Church | Church / Chapel | Exterior structure; roamer staging area |

### 1F (Ground Floor) — Verified
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Bar | Bar | Bomb site (3A) — social bar with counter |
| Stage | Stage / Strip Club | Bomb site (3B) — performance space |
| Kitchen | Kitchen / Kit | Food prep; utility room |
| Bathroom | Bathroom / Bath | Utility restroom |
| Break Room | Break / Break Room | Staff break area |
| Main Entrance | Entrance / Main / Foyer | Primary entry point; camera position |
| Lounge | Lounge | Transition/social area |
| Corridor | 1F Hall / Hallway / Corridor | Main 1F circulation |
| Garage | Garage / Garage Room | Vehicle storage; attacker spawn access |

### Basement (B) — Verified
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Arsenal | Arsenal / Armory / Arms | Bomb site (4A) — weapons storage |
| Garage (Basement) | Garage / Garage Base | Bomb site (4B) — basement vehicle storage |
| Basement Stairs | Basement Stairs / Down | Vertical descent — tight chokepoint |
| Basement Corridor | Basement Hall / Base Hall | Main basement passage |
| Electrical | Electrical / Elec | Utility room |
| Storage | Storage / Base Storage | Basement storage |

### Exterior / Attacker Spawns
| Location | Callout | Notes |

| Main Gate | Main Gate / Main | Spawn location |
| Shipping Dock | Shipping Dock / Dock | Spawn location |
| Warehouse | Warehouse / Ware | Spawn location |
| Construction Site | Construction / Con Site | Spawn location |
| Rooftop | Roof | Minimal space; roamer path via Church |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Gym entry (2F):** Control access to Gym/Bedroom site; funnel attacker approach
- **Bedroom to Gym wall (2F):** Segment the northwest site; provide anchor depth
- **Cash Room entry (2F):** Fortress-like; heavily reinforce to maintain security
- **CCTV to Cash Room wall (2F):** Segmentation wall; maintain anchor strategy
- **Bar entry (1F):** Control flow into Bar/Stage; reinforce for difficult attacker entry
- **Stage to Bar wall (1F):** Segment the 1F site; support anchor positioning
- **Basement Stairs entry wall:** Prevent easy basement descent; heavily defender-favored chokepoint
- **Arsenal / Garage division wall (Basement):** Segment basement site; allow defenders to hold separately

### Key Soft Walls
- **2F floor above Basement:** Multiple soft points enable vertical pressure from below
- **Gym exterior wall (2F):** Soft breaches create new entry angles and plant spots
- **1F floor above Basement:** Vertical destruction from Kitchen/Break Room areas enables basement pressure
- **Bar to adjacent room walls (1F):** Soft breaches create new plant angles
- **Church ceiling/floor:** Vertical destruction unique to Church area; creates special roamer plays
- **Basement walls:** Multiple soft walls allow roamer flanking routes

### Key Hatches (7 total)
- **Main Stairs hatch (2F to 1F):** Central vertical passage; primary roamer interception point
- **Basement Stairs hatch (1F Garage to Basement):** Vertical descent; heavily defender-controlled chokepoint
- **Church area hatch:** Unique vertical connection from Church; special vertical plays
- **Gym/Bedroom floor hatches (2F to 1F):** Vertical connections for attacker pressure
- **Additional hatches:** Multiple vertical passages on map; exact locations verify in-game

---

name: Consulate
overviewContent:
# Consulate — Overview

---

## Summary

Consulate is a three-story diplomatic/administrative building with unique courtyard-accessible interior areas. The map features 4 bomb sites split across three floors: CEO/Meeting and Admin/TV on 2F, Piano/Lounge on 1F, and Drug Lab/Armory in the basement.

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Rooms | Floor | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|-------|
| **Site 1** | CEO / Meeting | 2F | Large office + meeting room. Two-room site with open spacing. |
| **Site 2** | Admin / TV | 2F | Administrative offices. Smaller, compartmentalized setup. |
| **Site 3** | Piano / Lounge | 1F | Music room + seating. Furniture-heavy, moderate cover. |
| **Site 4** | Drug Lab/Armory | B | Basement site. Isolated, defender-biased. |

---

## Map Characteristics

### Vertical Structure
- **2F:** Two bomb sites (CEO/Meeting, Admin/TV) separated by Connector/Long Desk area. Visa Stairs is primary vertical descent point.
- **1F:** Piano/Lounge (west side), Visa Hall/Circle (central), Zulu (south). Bottom Spiral Stairs provide central vertical access.
- **Basement:** Two separate bomb sites (Drugs, Armory) connected via Red corridor. Kitchen/Cafeteria is north attacker entry point.

### Key Features
- **Courtyard element:** Central courtyard provides unique exterior-interior pressure routes (not heavily detailed in floor plan but noted in Consulate.md).
- **Split 2F sites:** Two distinct bomb sites on same floor creates flexible defender positioning but requires multi-site pressure management by attackers.
- **Basement isolation:** Drug Lab/Armory basement site is isolated with limited entry routes—defender-favored area.
- **Spiral Stairs:** Bottom Spiral provides central vertical axis for multi-floor coordination.

---

## Strategic Notes

### Attack Perspective
- Multi-site pressure across floors stretches defender roam resources.
- Basement sites can often be ignored early—focus on upper floors first.
- Visa Stairs is primary vertical chokepoint (defender-held).
- Courtyard routes create non-traditional entry angles (monitor for unexpected defender positioning).

### Defense Perspective
- Roamer cascade through Connector and Spiral Stairs critical for upper floor defense.
- 2F split sites (CEO/Meeting vs. Admin/TV) require careful anchor positioning.
- Basement is naturally defensible if isolated early; commit minimal resources there initially.
- Exterior courtyard defense critical but less understood (poor team WR suggests knowledge gap).

---

name: Oregon
overviewContent:
# Oregon — Overview

⚠️ **Y11S1 REWORK NOTICE:** Oregon was modernized in Operation Silent Hunt (Y11S1). Room names and layout below are based on verified callouts and floor plan images. Verify in-game before relying in match.

**Environment:** Classic militia compound; rustic house with barn structure and underground utility areas
**Floors:** Basement / Underground (B), Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor / Attic (2F)
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season only. Exits at Y11S1 mid-season swap (replaced by undisclosed maps).
**Year Added / Reworked:** Original map (Y1S0); major rework Operation Void Edge (Y5S2, March 2020); Y11S1 modernization rework

---

## Overview

Oregon is a classic competitive map featuring a main residential building with attached barn/utility structure and basement areas. The tight, close-quarters layout favors defenders with well-coordinated positioning and roaming. Multiple soft walls enable flexible rotations and site setup variations. The map's iconic design features distinct elevation changes, tight hallways, and limited long sightlines. Attackers require methodical clear operations and coordinated site execution.

Oregon has been a strong performer for the stack historically, with positive win rates across most players. However, **Grant's recent priorities flag Oregon as a WEAK map this season (6M/33.3% WR).** Despite the Y10S4 strong historical record, the current team performance has deteriorated. **Do NOT auto-protect Oregon in bans.** Reassess ban strategy based on current meta and team comfort.

One partial strategy developed (likely ATK_Attic_Bedroom from STACK_03 inline content, noting spawn peek vulnerability and Grant death during strat dev). Three bomb sites remain underdeveloped. Recommend documenting strategies with emphasis on Attic spawn peek mitigations.

---

## Bomb Sites

### Site 1 — Main Dorms Hall / Kids Dormitory — 2F
**Rooms:** Main Dorms Hall (1A — main dormitory) / Kids Dormitory (1B — children's bedroom)
**Description:** Upper floor site with iconic spawn peek vulnerability documented in strat dev. Main Dorms Hall is spacious with multiple beds and furniture providing cover; Kids Dormitory is smaller bedroom area. Multiple windows create significant external pressure. Attic connection provides rooftop/exterior approach vulnerability. Site is frequently attacked and heavily defended against.
**Common defense:** One anchor holds interior Main Dorms, with dedicated roamer holding Kids Dorm/Attic area for potential spawn peeks. Multiple soft wall rotations enable aggressive defender positioning. Window defense critical.
**Attack approach:** Account for spawn peek threat early in approach. Clear Kids Dorm/Attic carefully; prioritize window control before interior breach. Main Dorms Hall breach requires soft wall clearing and utility denial. Use utility to suppress defenders from window positions.
**⚠️ Spawn Peek Risk:** Documented vulnerability in existing partial strat. Grant died from spawn peek during strat development — flag this site as having spawn peek risk. Early utility (smokes, flashes) essential for approach safety.

### Site 2 — Dining Hall / Kitchen — 1F
**Rooms:** Dining Hall (2A — dining area) / Kitchen (2B — shared utility area)
**Description:** Ground floor site featuring dining and food preparation areas. Dining Hall is moderately open with dining furniture; Kitchen offers utility storage and tight angles. Central location with good rotation access through soft walls. Moderate attacker commitment required. Shared Kitchen area with alternate Meeting Hall site (Site 3).
**Common defense:** Anchor typically holds Kitchen for tight angles; one roamer plays adjacent Laundry/Bathroom for rotation denial. Soft wall reinforcement variable depending on site defense strategy and attacker location prediction.
**Attack approach:** Clear adjacent Laundry/Bathroom first to deny roamer flanks. Breach both Dining and Kitchen simultaneously or in quick succession. Multiple entry points from hallways and exterior access. Exterior breaches possible via barn/Watch Tower area.

### Site 3 — Meeting Hall / Kitchen — 1F
**Rooms:** Meeting Hall (3A — meeting/operations room) / Kitchen (3B — shared utility area)
**Description:** Alternate 1F bomb site pairing using shared Kitchen area with alternate primary room. Meeting Hall is a tactical operations/briefing room with different defensive angles than Dining Hall. Creates significant setup flexibility — both 1F sites share Kitchen but have different primary rooms. Attacker difficulty: early drone identification is critical to determine which 1F site is hit.
**Common defense:** Can anchor in Kitchen for either 1F site; Meeting Hall offers distinct defensive angles compared to Dining Hall. Roamer coverage varies based on which site is hit. Kitchen remains central defensive position.
**Attack approach:** Early drone identification critical to determine which 1F site is hit. Approach varies significantly — Dining Hall requires Laundry approach while Meeting Hall has different corridor vectors. Kitchen clearing must be flexible for both site possibilities.

### Site 4 — Laundry Room / Supply Room — Basement
**Rooms:** Laundry Room (4A — laundry/utility) / Supply Room (4B — supply storage)
**Description:** Basement level site with tight corridors and limited visibility. Laundry Room offers utility focus; Supply Room is storage-focused. Underground positioning makes defender rotations to upper floors time-consuming and resource-intensive. Multiple hatch access from above creates strong vertical vulnerability.
**Common defense:** Anchor holds basement area, typically consolidates between Laundry/Supply rooms. Roamer plays upper floor basement stairs/entry points for vertical pressure denial. Communication critical due to vertical isolation.
**Attack approach:** Vertical pressure from above is major advantage — hatches should be primary entry vector. Target basement stairs entry early to secure access routes. Coordinate simultaneous hatches with ground level basement stairs approach for split defense. Underground nature enables rapid site takeover if vertical control established.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Upper Floor / Attic) — Verified
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Main Dorms Hall | Main Dorms / Dorms | Bomb site (1A); upper floor dormitory; spawn peek vulnerability |
| Kids Dormitory | Kids / Kids Dorm | Bomb site (1B); children's bedroom; adjacent to Main Dorms |
| Attic | Attic | Upper floor storage; exposed windows; CRITICAL spawn peek position |
| Hallway | Hall / Hallway / 2F Hall | Main 2F circulation corridor |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility room; central to multiple areas |
| Stairs | Stairs / Main Stairs | Primary vertical access to 1F |

### 1F (Ground Floor) — Verified
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Dining Hall | Dining / Dining Room | Bomb site (2A); primary dining area |
| Meeting Hall | Meeting / Meeting Room | Bomb site (3A); alternate 1F site; meeting/operations room |
| Kitchen | Kitchen / Kit | Bomb site (2B, 3B); shared between both 1F sites; central utility |
| Laundry | Laundry / Wash | Common roamer position; 1F utility area |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Utility restroom; central to Kitchen/Dining area |
| Living Area | Living / Main | Central living space; circulation hub |
| Hallway | Hall / Main Hall | Central circulation corridor |
| Basement Stairs | Basement Stairs / Down | Vertical descent to Basement; tight chokepoint |
| Exterior (barn area) | Barn / Outside | Attached barn structure; exterior pressure |
| Watch Tower | Watch Tower / Tower | Exterior tower structure; rappel access |

### Basement / Underground (B) — Floor Plan Labeled
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Laundry Room | Laundry / Wash | Bomb site (4A); basement laundry/utility |
| Supply Room | Supply | Bomb site (4B); supply storage; hatch from Kitchen above |
| Basement Hall / Basement Corridor | Basement / Base Corridor | Main basement circulation |
| Utility Area | Utility / Utilities / Electric | Support systems area |
| Storage | Storage | Equipment storage basement area |
| Back Stairs | Back Stairs | Rear stairwell; basement to exterior |

⚠️ **Floor Plan Basement Labels:** Basement image shows many utility area names (Pillar, Boiler, Electric, Bunker, Barrels, Closet, Back Laundry, Laundry Storage, Freezer, Freezer Stairs). Reconcile these floor plan labels against verified in-game callouts — Y11S1 rework may have changed naming.

### Exterior / Attacker Spawns
| Location | Callout | Notes |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Main front | Front / Outside | Primary exterior entrance |
| Back area | Back / Rear | Rear of main building |
| Barn exterior | Barn | Attached barn/utility structure |
| Watch Tower | Watch Tower / Tower | Tall external structure with rappel points |
| Side areas | Side / Left / Right | Building sides |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Attic / Bedroom wall (2F):** Typically reinforced to separate objectives; controls sight lines between rooms
- **Kitchen / Dining connecting wall (1F):** Variable reinforcement — often left soft for roamer rotation flexibility
- **Kitchen / Meeting connecting wall (1F):** Similar reinforcement strategy; depends on which 1F site is hit
- **Basement stair entry wall (1F → B):** Reinforced to deny direct vertical line of sight and slow basement entry
- **Laundry / Supply division wall (Basement):** Reinforced to create defensive compartments

### Key Soft Walls
- **Hallway walls (all floors):** Multiple soft wall options enabling roamer rotation and attacker breach points
- **Laundry walls (1F):** Soft walls create roamer movement flexibility and attacker site isolation
- **Bathroom walls (1F/2F):** Multiple soft options for rotation enhancement
- **Basement corridor walls (B):** Soft walls enable multi-vector basement approaches

### Key Hatches
- **2F to 1F hatch (main stairwell):** Primary vertical connection; contested hatch position
- **1F to Basement hatch (Kitchen area):** Direct basement access; critical for vertical pressure coordination
- **Additional basement hatches:** Floor plan shows multiple connections; verify exact locations in-game

---

name: Skyscraper
overviewContent:
# Skyscraper — Overview

---

## Summary

Skyscraper is a compact, vertical-focused map featuring a Japanese high-rise building with rooftop dominance and window-based entry vectors. The map emphasizes exterior rooftop pressure balanced against interior anchor positions. Four bomb sites split across two floors: Tea Room/Karaoke and Exhibition/Office on 2F, Kitchen/BBQ and Bedroom/Bathroom on 1F.

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Rooms | Floor | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|-------|
| **Site 1** | Tea Room / Karaoke | 2F | Japanese hospitality + entertainment. Window-vulnerable site. |
| **Site 2** | Exhibition / Office | 2F | Art gallery + office. Less exterior pressure post-rework. |
| **Site 3** | Kitchen / BBQ | 1F | Food service + grilling. Moderate exterior window access. |
| **Site 4** | Bedroom / Bathroom | 1F | Residential + utility. Exterior pressure reduced post-rework. |

---

## Map Characteristics

### Vertical Structure
- **2F:** Two bomb sites (Tea/Karaoke west, Exhibition/Office east) with central Study/Storage/Hallway connecting area.
- **1F:** Two bomb sites split (Kitchen/BBQ north, Bedroom/Office south) with central Museum/Entrance circulation.
- **Rooftop:** Extensive exterior rooftop with zipline, rappel routes, and window access points.

### Key Features
- **Rooftop dominance:** Attackers can establish rooftop control early; extensive window breach points enable overwhelming vertical pressure.
- **Y5S4 rework impact:** Window removals on 2F Office, 1F Bedroom, and 1F Bathroom reduced exterior breach vulnerability. Balcony removal (west/north side) reduced runout exposure. Dragon Corridor (2F Shrine) added new narrow passage.
- **Window-heavy attack:** Many rooms have exterior windows; coordinating rooftop control with window breaches is primary attacker advantage.
- **Interior-focused defense:** Post-rework, defenders can rely more on interior anchoring; exterior pressure less omnipresent than original version.

name: Theme_Park
overviewContent:
# Theme Park — Overview

---

## Summary

Theme Park is a medium-sized abandoned amusement park with tight, linked corridors and multiple interconnected rooms across two main floors. The map features four bomb sites split across two floors: Office/Initiation and Bunk/Day Care on 2F, Armory/Throne and Lab/Storage on 1F. Y4S4 rework removed the rail platform and repositioned East/West sections, adding new main entrance (Gong) and new bomb sites (Armory/Throne).

---

## Bomb Sites

| Site | Rooms | Floor | Notes |
|------|-------|-------|-------|
| **Site 1** | Office / Initiation | 2F | Office + carnival entry. Two-room site with connected access. |
| **Site 2** | Bunk / Day Care | 2F | Sleeping quarters + activity area. Moderate anchor points. |
| **Site 3** | Armory / Throne | 1F | Weapons storage + carnival seating. New sites in Y4S4 rework. |
| **Site 4** | Lab / Storage | 1F | Research facility + storage. Interior-focused objective. |

---

## Map Characteristics

### Vertical Structure
- **2F:** Office/Initiation (east), Bunk/Day Care (west/central), connected via Top Yellow and Cafe Hall areas.
- **1F:** Armory/Throne (central), Lab/Storage (west/north). Dragon Stairs primary vertical descent.
- **Basement:** Connected via multiple vertical access points (Dragon Stairs, Arcade Stairs, Yellow Stairs).

### Key Features
- **Tight corridors throughout:** Theme Park emphasizes narrow hallways and confined spaces—defender-favorable geometry.
- **Multi-vertical access:** Three separate staircase routes (Dragon, Arcade, Yellow stairs) enable flexible attacker vertical pressure and defender roam coordination.
- **Linked room network:** Breakable wall connections (post-Y4S4) create rotations between Office/Initiation and Bunk/Day Care areas.
- **Carnival theming:** Unique carnival aesthetic creates distinctive room callouts (Gong, Bumper, Arcade, Throne, Dragon, etc.).
- **Rework modernization:** Y4S4 removal of rail platform opened sightlines, reduced spawnpeek vulnerability, repositioned East/West building closer together.

name: Villa
overviewContent:
# Villa — Overview

> ⚠️ **Note:** This document was generated before map images were available. Room names and bomb site descriptions below may contain outdated/incorrect names. For verified room names and adjacencies, use **VILLA_MAP_REFERENCE.md** as the authoritative source. Bomb site and callout corrections are noted inline below.

**Environment:** Italian country villa; classic competitive estate with courtyard and formal gardens
**Floors:** Basement / Wine Cellar (B), Ground Floor (1F), Upper Floor (2F)
**In Y10S4 Ranked Pool:** Yes
**In Y11S1 Ranked Pool:** ✅ Yes — first half of season only. Removed at Y11S1 mid-season swap (replaced by Favela + Stadium Bravo).
**Year Added / Reworked:** Added Y3S4 (2019); reworked Y6S3 for balance; refinements in Y9+; modernization rework in Y11S1 (lighting, geometry, and texture updates per Operation Silent Hunt patch notes)

---

## Overview

Villa was the stack's strongest-performing map in Y10S4 (Grant 75%, Sarge 100%, Hound 85.7%, Smigs 100%, Slug 70%). **Y11S1 data reversed this completely** — all players are below 50% in Y11S1, making Villa the team's ❌ Avoid map. Villa has also exited the ranked pool at mid-season swap. This folder remains as a contingency reference in case Villa returns in a future season. The Italian villa setting features a courtyard, formal gardens, and interior spaces with good rotation flexibility. Multiple floors create vertical gameplay opportunities with good hatch access. The map rewards coordinated team play and disciplined positioning.

---

## Bomb Sites

> ⚠️ **Original site names below were WRONG. Corrected verified names (from map images) are shown.**

### Site 1 — Planes/Aviator / Games/Pool — 2F
**Rooms:** Planes/Aviator and Games/Pool (bar area with pool table)
**Description:** Most played site on Villa (655 rounds). 60% defense WR — defender-sided. 2F west wing. Primary attacker breach: Classical Hall/West Hall → Games/Pool wall (the "main wall") + Vault wall via the 90-window (Hibana role — ⚠️ operator reassignment required with current roster). 3-man clears Study before the breach. Grant backside push from basement approach.
**Common defense:** Aruni gate on connecting doorframe, Kapkan in Gallery/Study approaches, Vigil roamer from balcony/North Hall. Team wins this site at very high rates.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Aviator_Games_Room.md.

### Site 2 — Trophy / Statue — 2F
**Rooms:** Trophy and Statue (2F north wing — above Kitchen/Dining)
**Description:** 51% defense WR (488 rounds) — most balanced site on Villa. Master Bedroom wall breach is the primary execute. Bedroom wall is almost always reinforced.
**Common defense:** Aruni on Trophy/Dining arch, Kapkan in Dining approach, Valkyrie cams, Vigil roamer in Dining/Living.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Trophy_Statuary.md.

### Site 3 — Living Room / Library — 1F
**Rooms:** Living Room and Library (1F west/south wing)
**Description:** 43% defense WR (58 rounds) — most attacker-favored site on Villa. Rarely picked by defenders. Vertical hatch pressure from 2F above is the win condition. Extremely limited team data.
**Common defense:** See DEF_Living_Library.md.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Living_Library.md.

### Site 4 — Kitchen / Dining — 1F
**Rooms:** Kitchen and Dining (1F north/right wing — connected internally via China)
**Description:** 60% defense WR (355 rounds) — defender-sided. Primary attacker breach: Laundry → Dining wall (east side) + vertical from 2F Astronomy/Statue-Connector area. Almost always appears as the third site option (third attack).
**Common defense:** See DEF_Kitchen_Dining.md.
**Attack approach:** See ATK_Kitchen_Dining.md.

---

## Room Names & Community Callouts

### 2F (Top Floor) — Verified names
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Planes/Aviator | Planes / Aviator | Bomb site (Site 1A) |
| Games/Pool | Pool / Games | Bomb site (Site 1B) — has bar counter |
| Vault | Vault | Former: Hunting Vault. North of both Site 1 rooms. |
| Classical Hall/West Hall | Classical / West Hall | Main approach wall into Games/Pool |
| Study | Study | East side — 3-man clear before Site 1 breach |
| 90 | 90 | Corner area; 90-window position (Hibana solo position — ⚠️ operator reassignment required) |
| North Hall | North Hall | Connecting hallway between both 2F wings |
| Landing/Top Red | Top Red / Landing | Top of Red Stairs on 2F |
| Top Main/Top Classic | Top Main / Top Classic | Top of Main Stairs on 2F |
| Trophy | Trophy | Bomb site (Site 2A) |
| Statue | Statue | Bomb site (Site 2B). Former: Statuary Room. |
| Sofa | Sofa | Area between Trophy and Statue |
| Connector / Connector Wolf | Connector / Wolf | North hub connecting Trophy/Statue wing |
| Astronomy | Astronomy | Former: Astronomy Room. Above Kitchen (1F). |
| Master Bedroom | Master / Master Bed | Adjacent to Statue; breach wall for Site 2 |
| Bathroom | Bathroom | Former: Master Bathroom |
| Closet | Closet | Former: Walk-in Closet |

### 1F (Ground Floor) — Verified names
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Kitchen | Kitchen / Kit | Bomb site (Site 4A) |
| Dining | Dining | Bomb site (Site 4B). Former: Dining Room. |
| China | China | Former: China Room. Connects Kitchen ↔ Dining. |
| Laundry | Laundry | Primary attacker breach approach to Site 4 |
| Memorial | Memorial | Former: Memorial Room. Secondary Dining approach. |
| Skylight Hall | Skylight | Former: Skylight Hallway. Central 1F corridor. |
| Back Hall | Back Hall | Former: Back Hallway. Approach to Kitchen west. |
| Astro Stairs | Astro Stairs | Stairs from Back Hall up to Astronomy (2F) |
| Living Room | Living | Bomb site (Site 3A) |
| Library | Library | Bomb site (Site 3B) |
| Gallery | Gallery | Between Library and Main Hall |
| Main Hall | Main Hall | Central horizontal corridor |
| Piano Hall | Piano Hall | Connects Main Hall to Piano/Main Stairs |
| Arches/Z | Arches / Z | Transition area between Dining wing and center |
| Cell/Prison | Cell / Prison | East of Memorial, near Red Stairs base |
| Mud Room/Bikes | Mud Room / Bikes | Former: Bicycle Storage |
| Red Stairs | Red Stairs | East side stairs (all floors) |

### Basement — Verified names
| Room | Callout | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| Wine | Wine | Former: Wine Cellar |
| Storage | Storage | Former: Art Storage |
| Old Office | Old Office | Right side basement |
| Garage | Garage | Exterior-accessible east side |
| Arch Hall | Arch Hall | Former: Arch Hallway. Central basement corridor. |
| Tasting | Tasting | Former: Tasting Room. South basement. |
| Bottom Pantry | Bottom Pantry | Base of Pantry Stairs |
| Bottom Back | Bottom Back | Base of Back Stairs in basement |

### Exterior
| Location | Community Callout | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Courtyard (front) | Courtyard / Front | Front courtyard approach |
| East Garden | East Garden / East | East side garden area |
| West side | West / West Garden | West side gardens |
| Back area | Back / Rear | Rear of villa |
| Balconies (2F) | Balcony / Balconies | Upper floor exterior balconies |
| Main entrance | Entrance / Front Door | Primary entry |
| Side approaches | Side / North / South | Building flanks |

---

## Key Walls & Hatches

### Critical Reinforced Walls
- **Master Bedroom / Bathroom wall (2F):** Typically reinforced to separate objectives; controls access between rooms
- **Statue / Aviator connecting wall (1F):** Reinforced to compartmentalize 1F objectives
- **Kitchen / Laundry connecting wall (1F):** Reinforced to separate utility areas and control site pressure
- **Wine Cellar / Basement wall (B):** Reinforced for basement compartmentalization
- **Study walls (2F):** Reinforced to deny easy rotation to Master Bedroom

### Key Soft Walls
- **Hallway walls (all floors):** Multiple soft wall options for roamer rotation and attacker breach points
- **Dining Room walls (1F):** Soft walls enable Kitchen/Dining/Aviator area connectivity and rotation
- **Gallery walls (2F):** Soft walls for rotation flexibility around upper floor
- **Basement corridor walls (B):** Soft wall options for vertical access support and basement navigation

### Key Hatches
- **2F to 1F hatch (main stairwell):** Primary vertical connection; enables upper site pressure from below
- **1F to Basement hatch (main circulation):** Primary basement access point; critical for vertical pressure
- **Secondary hatches:** Additional hatch options depending on room adjacencies ⚠️ Verify exact positions
- **Wine Cellar hatch (if applicable):** Direct basement objective access

---

## Camera Locations (Default)

- **Master Bedroom camera (2F):** Positioned in Master Bedroom or adjacent Gallery for upper site monitoring
- **Statue/Aviator camera (1F):** Positioned in Trophy Room or Aviator area for ground floor coverage
- **Kitchen area camera (1F):** Positioned to monitor Kitchen/Laundry site entry
- **Basement camera (B):** Positioned near Wine Cellar or main basement area for objective coverage
- **Courtyard camera:** Positioned to monitor exterior courtyard and main entrance approach
- **Valkyrie positioning:** Often placed on interior hallway overhangs, 2F balcony edges (if access), or tight corners for hard-to-destroy placement. Positioned to monitor roamer rotation paths and multi-floor pressure coordination.
- **Mozzie:** Typically deployed in main hallways (1F circulation, 2F hall), near site entries, basement hatch area, courtyard exterior, or garden area to watch attacker approach routes and multi-floor pressure

---

## Attack Strategies & Key Angles

**Courtyard Control:**
- Large courtyard exterior is distinctive feature — establish exterior dominance early
- Multiple exterior doors and windows create pressure vectors
- Garden area offers flanking pressure opportunities
- Exterior roamer can create meaningful pressure

**Multi-Floor Vertical Pressure:**
- Upper and lower sites benefit from vertical coordination
- Multiple hatch access points enable simultaneous vertical pressure
- Designate dedicated vertical player for hatch management
- Vertical pressure combined with ground-level attack overwhelming for defenders

**Adjacent Room Clears:**
- Study/Gallery clears before Master Bedroom breach prevent roamer flanks (upper)
- Dining/Living clears before Kitchen site prevent roamer ambush (lower)
- Methodical clear operations identify defender positions

**Site-Specific Approaches:**
- **Master/Bathroom:** Clear Study/Gallery first, then coordinated two-room breach
- **Statue/Aviator:** Clear Dining first, then multi-angle pressure on both 1F objective rooms
- **Kitchen/Laundry:** Clear Dining/Living first, then coordinate utility area breach with exterior garden pressure
- **Basement/Wine:** Heavy vertical pressure from above combined with ground-level basement stairs access

**Key angles to watch:**
- Courtyard approach angles (exterior roamer positions)
- Hallway junctions (roamer ambush points across floors)
- Hatch landing areas (vertical vulnerability)
- Soft wall breach points (defender counter-breach)
- Gallery/Study corners (upper roamer positions)
- Dining Room corners (lower roamer ambush)
- Stairwell transitions (vertical control points)
- Balcony edges (exterior roamer positions)

**Drone usage:**
- Scout courtyard/exterior first for roamer threat assessment
- Scout upper floors for Master/Study/Gallery roamer locations
- Reserve drones for mid-round site execute intel

---

## Defense Strategies & Anchor Spots

**Anchor Positioning (per site):**
- **Master Bedroom / Bathroom (2F):** Anchor holds Master Bedroom for spacious position; Bathroom offers tight alternative. Roamer plays Study/Gallery for flank denial. Strong rotation access to adjacent areas.
- **Statue / Aviator (1F):** Anchor can hold either room; flexibility between Trophy Room gallery angles or Aviator Bar counter defense. Roamer plays Dining Room for Kitchen-side flank. Good interior support access.
- **Kitchen / Laundry (1F):** Anchor holds Kitchen for utility positioning. Roamer plays Dining/Living areas for flanking denial. Vulnerable to garden area exterior pressure but manageable.
- **Basement / Wine Cellar (B):** Anchor consolidates in basement area; isolated from upper floors. Roamer must monitor upper floor hatch areas for vertical pressure. Communication critical.

**Roamer Routes:**
- **Upper roamer (2F):** Plays Study/Gallery area and hallway junctions; manages interior pressure denial for upper sites
- **Primary roamer (1F):** Plays Dining Room and hallway areas; rotates to flank Kitchen site or Statue/Aviator approach
- **Secondary roamer (if applicable):** Plays Garden/exterior area for exterior pressure denial
- **Vertical roamer:** Manages stairwell areas and hatch positions, denies vertical pressure coordination
- **Basement roamer:** Advanced positioning in basement for Lab/Wine cellar site support and vertical hatch denial

**Roaming Coordination:**
- Villa's soft wall options enable multiple roamer rotation paths
- Upper and lower floors have distinct roamer strategies
- Courtyard exterior requires attention despite interior focus
- Vertical hatch monitoring is critical; designate specific roamer hatch responsibility

---
